_comment: MAX_WORKFORCE_CSV_ROWS + 1 (15,001) valid rows, month 2027-02 -- same national_id prefix range as max-rows.csv, one row longer. The row cap is enforced at the ROUTE (POST /import/workforce/:month via routes/workforce.ts's own rowCount > MAX_WORKFORCE_CSV_ROWS check BEFORE beginImportTask/enqueueWorkforceImport are ever called), not inside WorkforceImportService.importCsv/parseWorkforceCsv themselves -- calling importCsv directly with this file will NOT throw (parseWorkforceCsv has no row cap of its own); only the HTTP route rejects it. This fixture is intended for route-level (supertest) and load-test use, proving the boundary is enforced at exactly the cap + 1, not off-by-one in either direction.
call: POST /import/workforce/:month (route-level -- this is where the row cap is actually enforced, NOT WorkforceImportService.importCsv)
